us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, Threats, and Toll Talk
us-politics.body:
Trump held a news conference doubling down on a Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet U.S. demands, threatening strikes on civilian infrastructure if no deal is reached. He invoked God's support for the U.S. cause and brushed off questions about potential war crimes. The Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine, offering no explanation.

Trump also floated charging tolls for passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a move that would affect global oil shipping. OPEC+ separately agreed to boost output once the strait reopens. A House Democrat moved to impeach Hegseth over the Iran conflict, while supporters argue the administration is applying necessary pressure after years of failed diplomacy.

sources.title: Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran

Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war

us-politics.headline: Hegseth Reshapes Pentagon, Faces Impeachment Push
us-politics.body:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and replaced them with Hillsdale College, a conservative school with no accredited law or medical programs. Supporters call it a correction of ideological bias in officer training. Critics see it as politicizing the military.

Separately, Rep. Ansari introduced articles of impeachment against Hegseth, citing his conduct around the Iran war. The move has little chance of success in the Republican-controlled House but signals growing Democratic pressure. Far-right commentator Laura Loomer also claimed credit for the arrest of a former Iranian commander's niece in California, a claim that drew scrutiny over its accuracy.

sources.title: House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale
Loomer takes credit for arrest of former Iranian commander’s niece in California

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Courts, Targets Biden Legacy
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariff cases, accusing justices of overreach. The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had adopted gender identity protections, reversing Biden-era policy. The EPA also took an early step toward rolling back methane flaring rules.

At the White House Easter Egg Roll, Trump mocked Biden over autopen use, drawing laughs from supporters and criticism from Democrats who called it petty. The administration's broad regulatory rollback is cheered by conservatives as restoring constitutional limits, while opponents argue it strips protections from vulnerable groups.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Trump roasts Biden over autopen use in viral interaction with kids at White House Easter Egg Roll

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape With Money and Primaries
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in 2026 Senate races, signaling an early and aggressive push to hold the chamber. In Montana, outgoing Sen. Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor. An AI-backed super PAC is also expanding into multiple GOP primaries after claiming wins in earlier contests.

On the Democratic side, Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger's poll numbers are sliding amid backlash over a gerrymandering dispute. New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew an immediate pushback from the Justice Department. The H2A agricultural visa debate is drawing rare bipartisan opposition, with both conservatives and liberals criticizing proposed changes to the farmworker program.

sources.title: GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested, faces five war crime murder charges
au-politics.body:
Former SAS soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and is expected to be charged with five counts of murder over alleged war crimes in Afghanistan. The charges follow years of civil and criminal investigations into conduct by Australian special forces during the Afghanistan conflict.

Roberts-Smith lost a defamation case in 2023 after a Federal Court found allegations of unlawful killings were substantially true. His arrest marks a significant escalation from civil findings to criminal prosecution. He has consistently denied wrongdoing.

sources.title: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier to be charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
Ben Roberts-Smith expected to be charged with war crimes

au-politics.headline: Fuel crisis drives EV surge as Albanese heads to Singapore
au-politics.body:
Prime Minister Albanese is travelling to Singapore to secure Australia's fuel supply as rising petrol prices bite households and businesses. Easter tourism delivered mixed results, with some regions reporting strong visitor numbers while others saw travellers cut trips short over fuel costs. Panic buying has prompted safety warnings, with petrol stations reporting customers arriving with everything from soft drink bottles to 200-litre drums.

Against that backdrop, Australians bought a record number of new electric vehicles in March. Analysts say fuel anxiety is accelerating a shift that government subsidies alone had not managed to drive.

sources.title: News live: Albanese to visit Singapore to shore up fuel supply; Australians buy record number of new EVs in March
Mixed results for tourism sector over Easter amid fuel price surge
EV sales surge as fuel anxiety clears auctions and showrooms in Australia
Coke bottles to a 200L drum: Petrol stations see dangerous fuel stockpiling

au-politics.headline: Trump's Iran rhetoric draws scrutiny as press freedom concerns mount
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump's public statements on Iran have shifted repeatedly, drawing criticism that the inconsistency is undermining US credibility on a conflict with direct consequences for global energy markets - including Australia's fuel supply. Analysis from ABC Australia argues the shifting rhetoric is eroding trust among allies and adversaries alike.

Separately, Trump has threatened to jail the journalist who broke the story of a US military rescue mission inside Iran. Press freedom advocates say the threat is part of a broader pattern of hostility toward reporting on sensitive national security matters. The White House has not backed down.

sources.title: Donald Trump's shifting Iran war rhetoric is working against his credibility
Trump threatens to jail journalist who broke story of US rescue mission in Iran

au-politics.headline: Bondi nanny extradited to Chile over Pinochet-era kidnapping claims
au-politics.body:
Adriana Rivas, a former Sydney nanny accused of being an agent of Augusto Pinochet's secret police, has lost her bid to avoid extradition and will be sent to Chile to face kidnapping charges. Rivas has lived in Australia for decades and denies the allegations, which relate to the forced disappearance of political opponents during Chile's military dictatorship in the 1970s.

Australian courts rejected her final legal challenge. Chilean authorities allege she participated in the abduction and torture of detainees. Human rights groups have long pushed for her extradition.

sources.title: Ex-Bondi nanny fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims
Accused Pinochet agent turned Bondi nanny Adriana Rivas to be extradited to Chile

world.headline: Iran War Escalates as Trump's Hormuz Deadline Approaches
world.body:
US and Israeli strikes on Iran are intensifying, with Trump threatening to hit power plants and warning of devastating attacks if Tehran refuses a deal over the Strait of Hormuz. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed the pace of strikes is increasing. Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant has already been targeted.

Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in retaliatory strikes on Israel, widening the conflict across the region. A US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran in a covert operation, and Trump threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of the mission.

Iran shows little sign of capitulating. Analysts question why Tehran would accept a ceasefire given what they describe as a poor track record from Washington and Jerusalem. North Korea, meanwhile, is keeping its distance from Iran.

sources.title: Trump’s threat to strike Iran’s power plants looms
‘Why would Iran agree to a ceasefire given US and Israeli track record?’
Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Clock ticks on Trump's Iran ultimatum with little sign of breakthrough

world.headline: Israeli Strikes Deepen Lebanon Crisis, Gaza Death Toll Rises
world.body:
Israel has intensified its ground offensive in Lebanon while continuing strikes in Gaza, killing at least ten Palestinians near a school. Israeli forces also fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron. Analysts say the attacks are deliberately designed to fracture Lebanon's internal political cohesion.

In Tel Aviv, protesters stormed the US embassy demanding an end to the wars. The demonstrations reflect growing domestic pressure inside Israel as the multi-front conflict stretches on with no clear resolution in sight.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say

world.headline: Vance Flies to Budapest to Boost Orban Before Sunday Vote
world.body:
Vice President JD Vance is travelling to Hungary ahead of Sunday's election to publicly back Viktor Orban's re-election bid. The visit signals an unusually direct intervention by a sitting US administration in a European ally's domestic politics.

Orban, who has long cultivated ties with Trump's circle, faces a tightening race. Vance's appearance is intended to shore up the incumbent's nationalist base and reinforce the transatlantic right-wing alignment that has defined US-Hungary relations since Trump returned to office.

sources.title: JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sets New Record for Farthest Human Travel
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II astronauts have broken the record for the farthest distance humans have ever travelled from Earth, surpassing the mark set during the Apollo programme over five decades ago. The milestone comes as the crew conducts a lunar flyby mission ahead of a planned crewed Moon landing.

The achievement marks a concrete step forward in the US effort to return humans to the Moon and compete with China's advancing space programme, which has set its own ambitious lunar goals for the late 2020s.

sources.title: Artemis II astronauts break record for farthest human travel from Earth
China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead

ai.headline: OpenAI insiders revolt as Altman's leadership faces scrutiny
ai.body:
Trust in Sam Altman is fracturing inside OpenAI, with insiders describing the CEO as the core problem facing the company. The criticism comes as OpenAI simultaneously tries to project confidence about its economic vision - publishing proposals for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek as AI displaces workers.

Meanwhile, a group of OpenAI alumni have been quietly deploying a fund that could reach $100 million, suggesting senior talent is already hedging its bets. The internal tensions sit awkwardly alongside OpenAI's geopolitical exposure: Iran has threatened the company's Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi, adding a security dimension to an already complicated moment for the organisation.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Robotics hits production reliability as AI hardware bets deepen
ai.body:
A new general-purpose robotics model called GEN-1 has reached 99% reliability across tasks ranging from folding boxes to repairing vacuums - a threshold that matters because it moves AI-driven robots from lab curiosity to factory floor candidate. The result signals that physical AI is closing the gap with software-only systems faster than most expected.

Anthropologic is reinforcing the infrastructure layer behind such advances, expanding its partnership with Google and Broadcom to secure next-generation compute. The pairing of reliable robot models with dedicated silicon pipelines suggests the industry is building toward deployment at scale, not just capability demonstrations.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: AI floods music charts and consulting decks simultaneously
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, having never performed live or existed as a human being. The chart takeover is a stress test the music industry did not prepare for, and it is happening in public.

On the business side, Indian startup Rocket is pitching AI-generated strategy reports styled after McKinsey deliverables, at a fraction of the consulting fee. Both stories point to the same pressure: AI is not just automating back-office tasks but moving into creative and knowledge-work outputs that were considered durable. MIT Technology Review argues that granular job-level data - not broad employment statistics - is the only honest way to measure what is actually being displaced.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

ai.headline: Vibe coding backlash grows as developer tools multiply
ai.body:
A pointed essay circulating on Hacker News argues that the enthusiasm around vibe coding - using AI to generate code with minimal oversight - has become a cult that mistakes speed for quality. The critique lands as the tooling ecosystem keeps expanding anyway: Freestyle launched sandboxes built specifically for coding agents, and Ghost Pepper offers local, offline speech-to-text for developers on macOS.

Separately, Solod emerged as a Go subset that compiles to C, targeting developers who want performance without leaving familiar syntax. The gap between the tools being built and the discipline required to use them well is widening. That tension is unlikely to resolve itself quietly.

sources.title: The cult of vibe coding is dogfooding run amok
Launch HN: Freestyle – Sandboxes for Coding Agents
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS
Solod – A Subset of Go That Translates to C

btc.headline: Bitcoin Slides Toward $68K as Whales Exit and Options Warn
btc.body:
Bitcoin is under pressure, dropping toward $68,000 as demand weakens and large holders sell. The options market had already flagged the move, quietly pricing in significant downside risk before the selloff materialized.

Macro tension is adding weight. Trump's Tuesday night deadline for an Iran deal has markets on edge, though Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana have so far held relatively steady against the geopolitical noise.

The bull case hasn't disappeared. Strategy is absorbing nearly three times the new Bitcoin supply entering the market, and some analysts still see a path to $110,000. But whale selling and softening demand are the story right now.

sources.title: Bitcoin, ether, solana hold steady as Trump sets Tuesday night deadline for Iran deal
Bitcoin drops toward $68,000 as demand weakens and whales sell
Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move

btc.headline: SEC's 'Reg Crypto' Advances as Safe Harbor Reaches White House
btc.body:
The SEC is close to releasing a dedicated regulatory framework for crypto fundraising, Chair Paul Atkins confirmed. The agency is calling it 'reg crypto,' modeled loosely on existing exemptions like Reg A and Reg D, and designed to give token issuers clearer rules for raising capital.

Separately, a broader crypto market safe harbor proposal has landed at the White House for final review. The back-to-back developments mark the most concrete regulatory momentum the industry has seen in years.

The shift in tone from the SEC under Atkins is sharp compared to the enforcement-first posture of recent years. Whether the frameworks hold up to legal scrutiny once published is the next question.

sources.title: Crypto market safe harbor lands at White House for review
SEC close to putting out 'reg crypto' for fundraising questions, Chair Atkins says

btc.headline: AI Firms Muscle Into Power Markets, Squeezing Bitcoin Miners
btc.body:
Bitcoin miners have a serious new rival for cheap electricity. Anthropic has signed a multi-gigawatt compute deal, putting AI companies in direct competition with miners for the same low-cost power infrastructure both industries depend on.

The dynamic has been building for months, but deals at this scale signal that AI demand is no longer a background pressure - it is a structural threat to miner margins. Power availability has long been the binding constraint for mining expansion, and losing ground to better-capitalized AI firms could reshape where and how miners operate.

JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon separately flagged blockchain and stablecoins as sources of 'new competition' for banks, underscoring how broadly the infrastructure battle is now being fought.

sources.title: Bitcoin miners face a new rival for cheap power as Anthropic signs multi-gigawatt compute deal
JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins

btc.headline: Aave Loses Risk Manager as DeFi Security Concerns Mount
btc.body:
Chaos Labs is stepping down as Aave's risk provider, ending a relationship central to how the protocol manages billions in lending exposure. The firm says the decision was not made quickly, but the exit lands amid a broader contributor exodus and unresolved internal disputes at Aave.

The timing is uncomfortable for DeFi broadly. The Solana Foundation is simultaneously rolling out new security tools and an incident response network as attacks on decentralized protocols continue. The two stories together point to a sector still struggling to professionalize its risk infrastructure.

Elsewhere, Polymarket overhauled its exchange, replacing USDC.e with a native USDC-backed token - a smaller but telling sign that DeFi platforms are tightening their underlying plumbing.

sources.title: Solana Foundation looks to beef up DeFi security as attacks continue
Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’
Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised white fish marinated in sweet miso paste and broiled until lacquered and tender
food.tip: Marinate the cod for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits, the deeper the umami and the better the glaze holds under the grill

exercise.name: Farmer's Carry Ladder
exercise.description: Walk 20 metres carrying heavy weights at your sides, rest 30 seconds, then increase load each round — brutally simple full-body conditioning that hammers grip, core, and posture simultaneously

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memory, and consciousness, spawning centuries of debate from Hobbes to Parfit.
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' argues personal identity is less fixed than we assume, which quietly dismantles how most people think about self-improvement, regret, and responsibility — unsettling and genuinely useful in equal measure
